notes: Snapshot schema identifier is structural configuration, not learned memory. Preserve any required schema/version contract outside the deletable snapshot before archival.
Controls how the summary snapshot is interpreted/rendered. If still needed after snapshot removal, it belongs with agent/procedural configuration rather than retrieved facts.
Stable agent identity. Must remain in the agent definition and should not depend on retrieval.
Snapshot build timestamp. Once snapshots are deleted, this derived maintenance timestamp has no semantic value.
Derived watermark for snapshot generation. Retrieval over JSONL truth should compute freshness from source logs/index fingerprint instead.
Derived count from the JSONL source. Future rebuild/stat commands can recompute it; it should not block snapshot deletion.

Contains provider/model thresholds/timeouts and counters for similarity-assisted compaction. Provider/model/min_confidence/timeout are configuration; operational counters are derived. Move only active configuration needed by the replacement retrieval system.
Contains learned decisions, key files, patterns, observations, questions, and overviews. Replace with retrieval over authoritative JSONL log entries.
State-file schema identifier. If state survives snapshot deletion, versioning belongs with the new state/index contract rather than the old snapshot state file.
Snapshot rebuild dirty bit. The new retrieval index should use its own fingerprint/rebuild lock state.
Operational status for snapshot generation. Superseded by retrieval index rebuild status/fingerprint.
Snapshot rebuild telemetry. Not needed for learned recall or procedural config.
Snapshot rebuild telemetry. Future index health should expose its own timestamps if needed.
